subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Prints a JSON formatted array of groups and users that would be synced to your Bitwarden organization

options.description: Test only the changes since the last successful sync

subcommands.name: sync
subcommands.description: Run a live sync operation and push data to your Bitwarden organization

subcommands.name: last-sync
subcommands.description: The last-sync command returns an ISO 8601 timestamp for the last sync operation that was performed for users or groups. You must specify either users or groups as an <object> to run the command agains

args.name: Object
args.description: You must specify either users or groups as an <object> to run the command against

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: The config command allows you to specify your directory settings

subcommands.name: server
subcommands.description: URL of your self-hosted installation (e.g. https://my.url.com) or https://vault.bitwarden.eu

args.name: directory-type
args.description:
Active Directory/LDAP : 0
Azure Active Directory : 1
Google Workspace/GSuite : 2
Okta : 3
OneLogin : 4

subcommands.name: ldap.password
subcommands.description: Password for connection to the LDAP server

args.name: key
args.description: Specify the Google Workspace/GSuite private key

args.name: value
args.description: Specify the value assigned to the setting

subcommands.name: clear-cache
subcommands.description: The clear-cache command allows you to clear cached data that the application stores while performing sync operations

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: The update command allows you to check if your Directory Connector CLI is up-to-date
